name: Language Server MCP
what_it_does: MCP server bridging Language Server Protocol (LSP) to MCP — enabling AI agents to use language server capabilities for code intelligence. Provides hover information, go-to-definition, code diagnostics, completion suggestions, and other LSP features to AI coding agents without requiring full IDE integration.
use_cases: Providing code diagnostics and type information from AI coding agents via LSP
Enabling go-to-definition and symbol lookup from code analysis agents
Using language server completions and hover info from AI code generation workflows
Bridging IDE-quality code intelligence to MCP-based agent development tools
not_for: Teams with IDE integration already providing sufficient code intelligence
Simple script editing without code intelligence needs
best_when: A developer building sophisticated AI coding agents needs IDE-quality code intelligence — using LSP servers to provide type info, diagnostics, and symbol navigation to agents working on complex codebases.
avoid_when: Simple file reading and grep-based search meets your needs. LSP bridge adds significant complexity — language servers must be installed and configured for each language.

auth.notes: Local LSP bridge — no authentication required. Requires language servers installed for target languages.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — LSP servers are typically open source
pricing.notes: Free open source LSP bridge MCP.

performance.rate_limits: Bounded by language server startup and indexing time

known_agent_gotchas: Language servers must be installed separately for each language — setup complexity is high
LSP initialization can be slow for large projects — implement appropriate startup timeouts
Language server capabilities vary — not all LSP features are supported by every language server
Community tool — LSP protocol bridging is complex; test thoroughly for your language/server combination
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local code analysis via LSP. No credentials. Source code processed by local language server. No external transmission.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local LSP. No credentials. Source code stays local. Low security risk.
